test_qa02_ec_phone_boundary_lengths
Test function
test_qa02_ec_phone_boundary_lengths
What it checks
Phone numbers at boundary lengths: form must accept the input without crashing. Default country is +966 Saudi Arabia which expects ~9 digits. Other lengths may legitimately disable Send Code as a validation signal — that is correct behavior, not a bug. We verify the form remains responsive and the input was accepted; we do NOT assert Send Code becomes enabled at every length.
Test data
7, min, 7, digits
Duration
3.59s
▶
✓

Failed: Test qa02 ec non numeric phone rejected
https://dev.mehadedu.com/en test_qa02_ec_non_numeric_phone_rejected 2026-06-01 17:01:59 15.00s
What this test was checking
EC-M-02: Non-numeric input (letters, symbols) must be rejected or ignored — verified against https://dev.mehadedu.com/en.
Test data used
fill("abc@test!")
Steps to Reproduce
  1. Open a browser and go to https://dev.mehadedu.com/en
  2. Enter a phone number in the WhatsApp number field
  3. Observe the result: it does NOT match what the test expected (see Actual Behavior)
What should happen
EC-M-02: Non-numeric input (letters, symbols) must be rejected or ignored.
What actually happened
The test could not load the page within 15000ms — the server is too slow or unreachable.
